## Envelopes Get Envelopes

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-by-account-id>`

Retrieves a list of envelopes that match your request. A large set of optional filters let you
filter by date, by envelope ID, or by status codes. Your request must include one or more of the
following parameters:

- `from_date`
- `envelope_ids`
- `transaction_ids`

Getting envelope status using `transaction_ids` is useful for offline signing situations where it
can be used determine if an envelope was created or not. It can be used for the cases where a
network connection was lost, before the envelope status could be returned.

To avoid unnecessary database queries, the DocuSign signature platform first checks requests to
ensure that the filter set supplied does not result in a zero-size response before querying the
database. For example, for a request with a `from_to_status` of `delivered` and a current `status`
of `created,sent`, DocuSign will always return an empty list. This is because the request translates
to: find the envelopes that were delivered between the `from_date` and `to_date` dates that have a
current status of `created` or `sent`. Since an envelope that has been delivered can never have a
status of `created` or `sent`, a zero-size response would be generated.

In this case, DocuSign does not query the database and returns an empty list immediately. The
following table shows the valid current envelope statuses (`status` parameter) for the different
status qualifiers (`from_to_status` parameter) in the request.

If the status and status qualifiers in the API request do not contain any of the values shown in the
Valid Current Statuses column, then an empty list is returned. Client applications should check that
the statuses (`status` parameter) they are requesting make sense for a given `from_to_status`
parameter value.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Status Qualifier (<code>from_to_status</code>)</th><th>Effective Status Qualifier</th><th>Valid Current Statuses</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>any (changed)</p></td><td><p>StatusChanged</p></td><td><p>any, created, sent, delivered, signed, completed, declined, voided, deleted</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>created</p></td><td><p>Created</p></td><td><p>any, created, sent, delivered, signed, completed, declined, voided, deleted</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>sent</p></td><td><p>Sent</p></td><td><p>any, sent, delivered, signed, completed, declined, voided, deleted</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>delivered</p></td><td><p>StatusChanged</p></td><td><p>any, delivered, signed, completed, declined, voided, deleted</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>signed</p></td><td><p>StatusChanged</p></td><td><p>any, signed, completed, declined, voided, deleted</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>completed</p></td><td><p>Completed</p></td><td><p>any, completed, declined, voided, deleted</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>declined</p></td><td><p>StatusChanged</p></td><td><p>any, declined, voided, deleted</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>timedout always return zero results</p></td><td><p>StatusChanged</p></td><td><p>any, voided, deleted</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>voided</p></td><td><p>Voided</p></td><td><p>any, voided, deleted</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>deleted</p></td><td><p>StatusChanged</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Extraneous results

In some cases, a request for a specific envelope status will include envelopes with additional
statuses. For example, in a request with a `from_date` of 2017-01-01, a `to_date` of 2017-01-07 and
the status qualifier (`from_to_status`) set to `delivered`, the response set might contain envelopes
that were created during that time period, but not delivered during the time period. As a
workaround, check the envelope status values in the result set as needed.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include purge information</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, information about envelopes that have been deleted is included in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Continuation token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A token returned in the response to a previous API call that is used to resume a search query from a specific point.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Number of items to return. Currently there is no implicit maximum limit of the number of items that can be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>From to status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelope status that you are checking for. Possible values are: - <code>Changed</code> (default) - <code>Completed</code> - <code>Created</code> - <code>Declined</code> - <code>Deleted</code> - <code>Delivered</code> - <code>Processing</code> - <code>Sent</code> - <code>Signed</code> - <code>TimedOut</code> - <code>Voided</code> For example, if you specify <code>Changed</code>, this method returns a list of envelopes that changed status during the <code>from_date</code> to <code>to_date</code> time period.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>email</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit results to envelopes sent by the account user with this email address. <code>user_name</code> must be given as well, and both <code>email</code> and <code>user_name</code> must refer to an existing account user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Search text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Free text search criteria that you can use to filter the list of envelopes that is returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ac status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the Authoritative Copy Status for the envelopes. The possible values are: - <code>Unknown</code> - <code>Original</code> - <code>Transferred</code> - <code>AuthoritativeCopy</code> - <code>AuthoritativeCopyExportPending</code> - <code>AuthoritativeCopyExported</code> - <code>DepositPending</code> - <code>Deposited</code> - <code>DepositedEO</code> - <code>DepositFailed</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma separated list of <code>envelopeId</code> values.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder types</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of folder types you want to retrieve envelopes from. Valid values are: - <code>normal</code> - <code>inbox</code> - <code>sentitems</code> - <code>draft</code> - <code>templates</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>powerformids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of <code>PowerFormId</code> values.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the user who created the envelopes to be retrieved. Note that an account can have multiple users, and any user with account access can retrieve envelopes by user_id from the account.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>block</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reserved for DocuSign.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Requester date format</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Requester date format</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns envelopes where the current user is the recipient, the sender, or the recipient only. (For example, <code>user_filter=sender</code>.) Valid values are: - <code>sender</code> - <code>recipient</code> - <code>recipient_only</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>order</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns envelopes in either ascending (<code>asc</code>) or descending (<code>desc</code>) order.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of current envelope statuses to included in the response. Possible values are: * <code>completed</code> * <code>created</code> * <code>declined</code> * <code>deleted</code> * <code>delivered</code> * <code>processing</code> * <code>sent</code> * <code>signed</code> * <code>timedout</code> * <code>voided</code> The <code>any</code> value is equivalent to any status.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Intersecting folder ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of folders that you want to get envelopes from. Valid values are: - <code>normal</code> - <code>inbox</code> - <code>sentitems</code> - <code>draft</code> - <code>templates</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query budget</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The time in seconds that the query should run before returning data.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Order by</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sorts results according to a specific property. Valid values are: - <code>last_modified</code> - <code>action_required</code> - <code>created</code> - <code>completed</code> - <code>envelope_name</code> - <code>expire</code> - <code>sent</code> - <code>signer_list</code> - <code>status</code> - <code>subject</code> - <code>user_name</code> - <code>status_changed</code> - <code>last_modified</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>include</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies additional information to return about the envelopes. Enter a comma-separated list, such as <code>tabs,recipients</code>. Valid values are: - <code>custom_fields</code>: The custom fields associated with the envelope. - <code>documents</code>: The documents associated with the envelope. - <code>attachments</code>: The attachments associated with the envelope. - <code>extensions</code>: Information about the email settings associated with the envelope. - <code>folders</code>: The folders where the envelope exists. - <code>recipients</code>: The recipients associated with the envelope. - <code>powerform</code>: The PowerForms associated with the envelope. - <code>payment_tabs</code>: The payment tabs associated with the envelope.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom field</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Specifies a envelope custom field name and value searched for in the envelopes. Format: <code>custom_envelope_field_name=desired_value</code> Example: If you have an envelope custom field named "Region" and you want to search for all envelopes where the value is "West" you would use set this parameter to <code>Region=West</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>From date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the date and time to start looking for status changes. This parameter is required unless <code>envelopeIds</code> or <code>transactionIds</code> are set. Although you can use any date format supported by the .NET system libraryâs [<code>DateTime.Parse()</code>][msoft] function, DocuSign recommends using [ISO 8601][] format dates with an explicit time zone offset If you do not provide a time zone offset, the method uses the serverâs time zone. For example, the following dates and times refer to the same instant: * <code>2017-05-02T01:44Z</code> * <code>2017-05-01T21:44-04:00</code> * <code>2017-05-01T18:44-07:00</code> [msoft]: <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.datetime.parse?view=net-10.0&amp;redirectedfrom=MSDN">https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.datetime.parse?view=net-10.0&amp;redirectedfrom=MSDN</a> [ISO 8601]: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601">https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601</a></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>exclude</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Excludes information from the response. Enter as a comma-separated list (, for example, <code>folders,powerforms</code>). Valid values are: - <code>recipients</code> - <code>powerforms</code> - <code>folders</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Last queried date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns envelopes that were modified prior to the specified date and time. Example: <code>2020-05-09T21:56:12.2500000Z</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the envelopes from specific folders. Enter as a comma-separated list of either valid folder Guids or the following values: - <code>awaiting_my_signature</code> - <code>completed</code> - <code>draft</code> - <code>drafts</code> - <code>expiring_soon</code> - <code>inbox</code> - <code>out_for_signature</code> - <code>recyclebin</code> - <code>sentitems</code> - <code>waiting_for_others</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cdse mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reserved for DocuSign.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limit results to envelopes sent by the account user with this user name. <code>email</code> must be given as well, and both <code>email</code> and <code>user_name</code> must refer to an existing account user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This value is supported and currently has no implicit maximum items.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transaction ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of envelope transaction IDs. Transaction IDs are only valid for seven days.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>To date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the date and time to stop looking for status changes. The default is the current date and time. Although you can use any date format supported by the .NET system libraryâs [<code>DateTime.Parse()</code>][msoft] function, DocuSign recommends using [ISO 8601][] format dates with an explicit time zone offset If you do not provide a time zone offset, the method uses the serverâs time zone. For example, the following dates and times refer to the same instant: * <code>2017-05-02T01:44Z</code> * <code>2017-05-01T21:44-04:00</code> * <code>2017-05-01T18:44-07:00</code> [msoft]: <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.datetime.parse?view=net-10.0&amp;redirectedfrom=MSDN">https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.datetime.parse?view=net-10.0&amp;redirectedfrom=MSDN</a> [ISO 8601]: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601">https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601</a></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Envelopes Get Envelope

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-by-account-id-envelope-id>`

Retrieves the overall status for the specified envelope. To get the status of a list of envelopes,
use
[Envelope: listStatusChanges](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/reference/envelopes/envelopes/liststatuschanges/)
.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced update</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When <strong>true</strong>, envelope information can be added or modified.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>include</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies additional information about the envelope to return. Enter a comma-separated list, such as <code>tabs,recipients</code>. Valid values are: - <code>custom_fields</code>: The custom fields associated with the envelope. - <code>documents</code>: The documents associated with the envelope. - <code>attachments</code>: The attachments associated with the envelope. - <code>extensions</code>: Information about the email settings associated with the envelope. - <code>folders</code>: The folder where the envelope exists. - <code>recipients</code>: The recipients associated with the envelope. - <code>powerform</code>: The PowerForms associated with the envelope. - <code>tabs</code>: The tabs associated with the envelope. - <code>payment_tabs</code>: The payment tabs associated with the envelope.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Comments Get Comments Transcript

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-comments-transcript-by-account-id-envelope-id>`

Retrieves a PDF file containing all of the comments that senders and recipients have added to the
documents in an envelope.

**Note**: Comments are disabled by default. To use the comments feature, an account administrator
must enable comments on the account (in the `accountSettingsInformation` object, set the
`enableSigningExtensionComments` property to **true**).

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/comments/transcript endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>(Optional) The encoding to use for the file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- DOCUSIGN:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- DOCUSIGN:SERVER_ERROR
- DOCUSIGN:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- DOCUSIGN:TIMEOUT
- DOCUSIGN:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- DOCUSIGN:UNAUTHORIZED
- DOCUSIGN:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Custom Fields Get Custom Fields

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-custom-fields-by-account-id-envelope-id>`

Retrieves the custom field information for the specified envelope. You can use these fields in the
envelopes for your account to record information about the envelope, help search for envelopes, and
track information. The envelope custom fields are shown in the Envelope Settings section when a user
is creating an envelope in the DocuSign member console. The envelope custom fields are not seen by
the envelope recipients. There are two types of envelope custom fields, text, and list. A text
custom field lets the sender enter the value for the field. With a list custom field, the sender
selects the value of the field from a premade list.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/custom_fields endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- DOCUSIGN:CLIENT_ERROR
- DOCUSIGN:CONNECTIVITY

## Documents Get Documents

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-documents-by-account-id-envelope-id>`

Retrieves a list of documents associated with the specified envelope. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/documents endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. ## Documents Get Document

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-documents-by-account-id-envelope-id-document-id>`

Retrieves the specified document from the envelope. If the account has the Highlight Data Changes
feature enabled, there is an option to request that any changes in the envelope be highlighted. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/documents/{documentId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter takes the following special keywords: - <code>combined</code>: Retrieves a PDF file that contains the combined content of all of the documents. If the account option <strong>Attach certification of completion to envelope</strong> is on, then the Certificate of Completion is also included in the PDF file. You set this account option in the Admin tool on the <strong>Signing Settings</strong> screen, or by setting the <code>attachCompletedEnvelope</code> property in the <code>accountSettings</code> object to <strong>true</strong>. - <code>archive</code>: Retrieves a ZIP archive that contains all of the PDF documents and the Certificate of Completion.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>certificate</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>false</strong>, the envelope signing certificate is removed from the download.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shared user ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of a shared user that you want to impersonate to retrieve their view of the list of documents. This parameter is used in the context of a shared inbox (i.e., when you share envelopes from one user to another through the RADmin console).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>encrypt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the PDF bytes returned in the response are encrypted for all the key managers configured on your DocuSign account. You can decrypt the documents by using the Key Manager DecryptDocument API method. For more information about Key Manager, see the DocuSign Security Appliance Installation Guide that your organization received from DocuSign.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reserved for DocuSign.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Documents by userid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, allows recipients to get documents by their user id. For example, if a user is included in two different routing orders with different visibilities, using this parameter returns all of the documents from both routing orders.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the language for the Certificate of Completion in the response. The supported languages are: Chinese Simplified (zh_CN), Chinese Traditional (zh_TW), Dutch (nl), English US (en), French (fr), German (de), Italian (it), Japanese (ja), Korean (ko), Portuguese (pt), Portuguese (Brazil) (pt_BR), Russian (ru), Spanish (es).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show changes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, any changed fields for the returned PDF are highlighted in yellow and optional signatures or initials outlined in red.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recipient ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Allows the sender to retrieve the documents as one of the recipients that they control. The <code>documents_by_userid</code> parameter must be set to <strong>false</strong> for this functionality to work.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>watermark</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the account has the watermark feature enabled, and the envelope is not complete, then the watermark for the account is added to the PDF documents. This option can remove the watermark.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Document Fields Get Document Fields

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-documents-fields-by-account-id-envelope-id-document-id>`

Retrieves the custom document field information from an existing envelope document. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/documents/{documentId}/fields endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The <code>documentId</code> is set by the API client. It is an integer that falls between <code>1</code> and 2,147,483,647. The value is encoded as a string without commas. The values <code>1</code>, <code>2</code>, <code>3</code>, and so on are typically used to identify the first few documents in an envelope. Tab definitions include a <code>documentId</code> property that specifies the document on which to place the tab.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/documents/{documentId}/html_definitions endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The <code>documentId</code> is set by the API client. ## Pages Get Page Images

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-documents-pages-by-account-id-envelope-id-document-id>`

Returns images of the pages in a document for display based on the parameters that you specify. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/documents/{documentId}/pages endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The <code>documentId</code> is set by the API client. It is an integer that falls between <code>1</code> and 2,147,483,647. The value is encoded as a string without commas. The values <code>1</code>, <code>2</code>, <code>3</code>, and so on are typically used to identify the first few documents in an envelope. Tab definitions include a <code>documentId</code> property that specifies the document on which to place the tab.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max width</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the maximum width of the returned images in pixels.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of results to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max height</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the maximum height of the returned images in pixels.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>dpi</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of dots per inch (DPI) for the resulting images. Valid values are 1-310 DPI. The default value is 94.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>nocache</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <strong>true</strong>, using cache is disabled and image information is retrieved from a database. <strong>True</strong> is the default value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show changes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <strong>true</strong>, changes display in the user interface.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The position within the total result set from which to start returning values. The value <strong>thumbnail</strong> may be used to return the page image.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Tabs Get Document Tabs

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-documents-tabs-by-account-id-envelope-id-document-id>`

This method returns the tabs associated with a document. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/documents/{documentId}/tabs endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The <code>documentId</code> is set by the API client. It is an integer that falls between <code>1</code> and 2,147,483,647. The value is encoded as a string without commas. The values <code>1</code>, <code>2</code>, <code>3</code>, and so on are typically used to identify the first few documents in an envelope. Tab definitions include a <code>documentId</code> property that specifies the document on which to place the tab.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page numbers</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters for tabs that occur on the pages that you specify. Enter as a comma-separated list of page GUIDs. Example: <code>page_numbers=2,6</code> Note: You can only enter individual page numbers, and not a page range.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Form Data Get Form Data

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-form-data-by-account-id-envelope-id>`

This method downloads the envelope and field data from any in-process, completed, or canceled
envelope that you sent or that is shared with you. Recipients who are also full administrators on an
account can view form data for any envelopes that another user on the account has sent to them.

**Note**: To use this feature, the Sending Setting "Allow sender to download form data" must be
enabled for the account.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/form_data endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Lock Get Envelope Lock

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-lock-by-account-id-envelope-id>`

Retrieves general information about an envelope lock. If the call is made by the locked by user and
the request has the same integrator key as original, then the `X-DocuSign-Edit` header and
additional lock information is included in the response. This information enables users to recover a
lost editing session token and the `X-DocuSign-Edit` header.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/lock endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. ## Recipients Get Recipients

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-recipients-by-account-id-envelope-id>`

Retrieves the status of all recipients in a single envelope and identifies the current recipient in
the routing list. This method can also be used to retrieve the tab values. The `currentRoutingOrder`
property of the response contains the `routingOrder` value of the current recipient indicating that
the envelope has been sent to the recipient, but the recipient has not completed their actions.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/recipients endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. ## Consumer Disclosure Get Consumer Disclosure Envelope Id Recipient Id

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-recipients-consumer-disclosure-by-account-id-envelope-id-recipient-id>`

Retrieves the default, HTML-formatted Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure (ERSD) for the
envelope that you specify. The default ERSD disclosure that DocuSign provides for the convenience of
U.S.-based customers only. This default disclosure is only valid for transactions between U.S.-based
parties. To set the language of the disclosure that you want to retrieve, use the optional
`langCode` query parameter.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/recipients/{recipientId}/consumer_disclosure
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recipient Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A local reference that senders use to map recipients to other objects, such as specific document tabs. Within an envelope, each <code>recipientId</code> must be unique, but there is no uniqueness requirement across envelopes. For example, many envelopes assign the first recipient a <code>recipientId</code> of <code>1</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Lang Code</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>(Optional) The code for the signer language version of the disclosure that you want to retrieve. The following languages are supported: - Arabic (<code>ar</code>) - Bulgarian (<code>bg</code>) - Czech (<code>cs</code>) - Chinese Simplified (<code>zh_CN</code>) - Chinese Traditional (<code>zh_TW</code>) - Croatian (<code>hr</code>) - Danish (<code>da</code>) - Dutch (<code>nl</code>) - English US (<code>en</code>) - English UK (<code>en_GB</code>) - Estonian (<code>et</code>) - Farsi (<code>fa</code>) - Finnish (<code>fi</code>) - French (<code>fr</code>) - French Canadian (<code>fr_CA</code>) - German (<code>de</code>) - Greek (<code>el</code>) - Hebrew (<code>he</code>) - Hindi (<code>hi</code>) - Hungarian (<code>hu</code>) - Bahasa Indonesian (<code>id</code>) - Italian (<code>it</code>) - Japanese (<code>ja</code>) - Korean (<code>ko</code>) - Latvian (<code>lv</code>) - Lithuanian (<code>lt</code>) - Bahasa Melayu (<code>ms</code>) - Norwegian (<code>no</code>) - Polish (<code>pl</code>) - Portuguese (<code>pt</code>) - Portuguese Brazil (<code>pt_BR</code>) - Romanian (<code>ro</code>) - Russian (<code>ru</code>) - Serbian (<code>sr</code>) - Slovak (<code>sk</code>) - Slovenian (<code>sl</code>) - Spanish (<code>es</code>) - Spanish Latin America (<code>es_MX</code>) - Swedish (<code>sv</code>) - Thai (<code>th</code>) - Turkish (<code>tr</code>) - Ukrainian (<code>uk</code>) - Vietnamese (<code>vi</code>) Additionally, you can automatically detect the browser language being used by the viewer and display the disclosure in that language by setting the value to <code>browser</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Consumer Disclosure Get Consumer Disclosure Envelope Id Recipient Id Lang Code

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-recipients-consumer-disclosure-by-account-id-envelope-id-recipient-id-lang-code>`

Retrieves the HTML-formatted Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure (ERSD) for the envelope
recipient that you specify. This disclosure might differ from the account-level disclosure, based on
the signing brand applied to the envelope and the recipientâs language settings. To set the language
of the disclosure that you want to retrieve, specify the `langCode` as either a path or query
parameter.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/recipients/{recipientId}/consumer_disclosure/{langCode}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Lang Code</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>(Optional) The code for the signer language version of the disclosure that you want to retrieve, as a path parameter. ## Recipients Get Recipient Document Visibility

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-recipients-document-visibility-by-account-id-envelope-id-recipient-id>`

This method returns information about document visibility for a recipient. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the

endpoint.

## Recipients Get Recipient Initials Image

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-recipients-initials-image-by-account-id-envelope-id-recipient-id>`

Retrieves the initials image for the specified user. The image is returned in the same format as it
was uploaded. In the request you can specify if the chrome (the added line and identifier around the
initial image) is returned with the image. The userId specified in the endpoint must match the
authenticated userâs user ID and the user must be a member of the account.

The `signatureIdOrName` paramter accepts signature ID or signature name. DocuSign recommends you use
signature ID (`signatureId`), since some names contain characters that do not properly URL encode.
If you use the user name, it is likely that the name includes spaces and you might need to URL
encode the name before using it in the endpoint. For example: "Bob Smith" to "Bob%20Smith" Older
envelopes might only contain chromed images. If getting the non-chromed image fails, try getting the
chromed image.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/recipients/{recipientId}/initials_image endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recipient Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A local reference that senders use to map recipients to other objects, such as specific document tabs. Within an envelope, each <code>recipientId</code> must be unique, but there is no uniqueness requirement across envelopes. For example, many envelopes assign the first recipient a <code>recipientId</code> of <code>1</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include chrome</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The added line and identifier around the initial image. Note: Older envelopes might only have chromed images. If getting the non-chromed image fails, try getting the chromed image.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Recipients Get Recipient Signature Image

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-recipients-signature-image-by-account-id-envelope-id-recipient-id>`

Retrieves the specified user signature image. The image is returned in the same format as uploaded.
In the request you can specify if the chrome (the added line and identifier around the initial
image) is returned with the image.

The userId specified in the endpoint must match the authenticated userâs user ID and the user must
be a member of the account.

The `signatureIdOrName` parameter accepts signature ID or signature name. DocuSign recommends you
use signature ID (`signatureId`), since some names contain characters that donât properly URL
encode. If you use the user name, it is likely that the name includes spaces and you might need to
URL encode the name before using it in the endpoint. For example: "Bob Smith" to "Bob%20Smith" Older
envelopes might only have chromed images. If getting the non-chromed image fails, try getting the
chromed image.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/recipients/{recipientId}/signature_image endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelope Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The envelopeâs GUID. Example: <code>93be49ab-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-f752070d71ec</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recipient Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A local reference that senders use to map recipients to other objects, such as specific document tabs. Within an envelope, each <code>recipientId</code> must be unique, but there is no uniqueness requirement across envelopes. For example, many envelopes assign the first recipient a <code>recipientId</code> of <code>1</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include chrome</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the response includes the chromed version of the signature image.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Recipients Get Recipient Tabs

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-recipients-tabs-by-account-id-envelope-id-recipient-id>`

Retrieves information about the tabs associated with a recipient. You can make a single API call to
get all the tab values and information from a given, completed envelope in addition to draft ones.
Tab values can be retrieved by using the EnvelopeRecipients:list method with query parameter
include_tabs set to "true".

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/recipients/{recipientId}/tabs endpoint.

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-templates-by-account-id-envelope-id>`

operation makes an HTTP GET request to the

## Envelope Transfer Rules Get Envelope Transfer Rules

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-envelopes-transfer-rules-by-account-id>`

This method retrieves a list of envelope transfer rules associated with an account.

**Note**: Only Administrators can create and use envelope transfer rules. In addition, to use
envelope transfer rules, the **Transfer Custody** feature must be enabled for your account.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/transfer_rules
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>(Optional) The maximum number of results to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>(Optional) The position within the total result set from which to start returning values. The value <strong>thumbnail</strong> may be used to return the page image.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Folders Get Folders

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-folders-by-account-id>`

Retrieves a list of the folders for the account, including the folder hierarchy. You can specify
whether to return just the template folder or template folder and normal folders by setting the
`template` query string parameter. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/folders endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Narrows down the resulting folder list by the following values: - <code>all</code>: Returns all templates owned or shared with the user. (default) - <code>owned_by_me</code>: Returns only templates the user owns. - <code>shared_with_me</code>: Returns only templates that are shared with the user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include items</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether folder items are included in the response. If this parameter is omitted, the default is false.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>include</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of folder types to include in the response. Valid values are: - <code>envelope_folders</code>: Returns a list of envelope folders. (Default) - <code>template_folders</code>: Returns a list of template folders. - <code>shared_template_folders</code>: Returns a list of shared template folders.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>template</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter is deprecated as of version 2.1. Use <code>include</code> instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The position within the total result set from which to start returning values.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/groups/{groupId}/brands endpoint.

operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/identity_verification
endpoint.

## Permission Profiles Get Permission Profiles

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-permission-profiles-by-account-id>`

This method returns a list of permission profiles that are associated with an account. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/permission_profiles endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>include</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of additional properties to return in the response. Valid values are: - <code>user_count</code>: The total number of users associated with the permission profile. - <code>closed_users</code>: Includes closed users in the <code>user_count</code>. - <code>account_management</code>: The account management settings. - <code>metadata</code>: Metadata indicating whether the properties associated with the account permission profile are editable.
