## Permission Profiles Get Permission Profile

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-permission-profiles-by-account-id-permission-profile-id>`

This method returns information about a specific permission profile that is associated with an
account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/permission_profiles/{permissionProfileId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Permission Profile Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the permission profile. ## Power Forms Get Power Form Data

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-powerforms-form-data-by-account-id-power-form-id>`

This method enables Powerform Administrators or the sender of a PowerForm to download the data that
recipients have entered into a PowerForm. You specify the format in which you want to retrieve the
data in the `Accept` header.

This header accepts the following values:

- `application/json`: JSON format
- `application/xml`: XML format
- `text/csv`: Comma-separated value (CSV) format

**Note**: Only PowerForm Administrators or the PowerForm Sender can download the data associated
with a PowerForm.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/powerforms/{powerFormId}/form_data endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Power Form Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the PowerForm.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Data layout</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The layout in which to return the PowerForm data. Valid values are: - <code>Native</code> - <code>Csv_Classic</code> - <code>Csv_One_Envelope_Per_Line</code> - <code>Xml_Classic</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>From date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The start date for a date range in UTC DateTime format. <strong>Note</strong>: If this property is null, no date filtering is applied.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>To date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The end date of a date range in UTC DateTime format. The default value is <code>UtcNow</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Recipient Names Get Recipient Names

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-recipient-names-by-account-id>`

Retrieves a list of all of the names associated with the email address that you pass in. This list
can include variants of a single recipient''s name that are used for signing, as well as the names
of multiple different recipients.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/recipient_names endpoint.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Search Folders Get Search Folder Contents

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-search-folders-by-account-id-search-folder-id>`

**This method is deprecated in API v2.1.** Use
[Envelopes::listStatusChanges](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/reference/envelopes/envelopes/liststatuschanges/)
instead. Retrieves a list of items that match the criteria specified in the query. If the user ID of
the user making the call is the same as the user ID for any returned recipient, then the userId
property is added to the returned information for those recipients. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/search_folders/{searchFolderId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Search Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the envelope group that is searched by the request. These are logical groupings, not actual folder names. Valid values are: drafts, awaiting_my_signature, completed, out_for_signature.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the number of records returned in the cache. The number must be greater than 0 and less than or equal to 100.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>all</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies that all envelopes that match the criteria are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include recipients</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the recipient information is returned in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>order</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the order in which the list is returned. Valid values are: <code>asc</code> for ascending order, and <code>desc</code> for descending order.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Order by</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the property used to sort the list. Valid values are: <code>action_required</code>, <code>created</code>, <code>completed</code>, <code>sent</code>, <code>signer_list</code>, <code>status</code>, or <code>subject</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>From date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the start of the date range to return. If no value is provided, the default search is the previous 30 days.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the starting location in the result set of the items that are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>To date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the end of the date range to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## BCCEmail Archive Get BCCEmail Archive List

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-settings-bcc-email-archives-by-account-id>`

This method retrieves all of the BCC email archive configurations associated with an account. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/settings/bcc_email_archives
endpoint.

## BCCEmail Archive Get BCCEmail Archive History List

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-settings-bcc-email-archives-by-account-id-bcc-email-archive-id>`

This method returns a specific BCC email archive configuration for an account, as well as the
history of changes to the email address. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/settings/bcc_email_archives/{bccEmailArchiveId} endpoint.

## Settings Get Settings

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-settings-by-account-id>`

Retrieves the account settings information for the specified account. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/settings endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Envelope Purge Configuration Get Envelope Purge Configuration

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-settings-envelope-purge-configuration-by-account-id>`

An envelope purge configuration enables account administrators to permanently remove documents and
their field data from completed and voided envelopes after a specified retention period
(`retentionDays`). This method retrieves the current envelope purge configuration for your account.

**Note**: To use this method, you must be an account administrator.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/settings/envelope_purge_configuration endpoint.

### Parameters

## Shared Access Get Shared Access

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-shared-access-by-account-id>`

Retrieves shared item status for one or more users and types of items. Users with account
administration privileges can retrieve shared access information for all account users. Users
without account administrator privileges can only retrieve shared access information for themselves,
and the returned information is limited to retrieving the status of the members of the account that
are sharing their folders to the user. This is equivalent to setting the `shared` parameter to
`shared_from`.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/shared_access endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the maximum number of results included in the response. If no value is specified, this defaults to 1000.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Search text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filter user names based on the specified string. The wild-card ''*'' (asterisk) can be used in the string.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>shared</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of sharing filters that specifies which users appear in the response. - <code>not_shared</code>: The response lists users who do not share items of <code>item_type</code> with the current user. - <code>shared_to</code>: The response lists users in <code>user_list</code> who are sharing items to current user. - <code>shared_from</code>: The response lists users in <code>user_list</code> who are sharing items from the current user. - <code>shared_to_and_from</code>: The response lists users in <code>user_list</code> who are sharing items to and from the current user. If the current user does not have administrative privileges, only the <code>shared_to</code> option is valid.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Envelopes not shared user status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This query parameter works in conjunction with <code>user_ids</code>. When you specify one of the following user statuses, the query limits the results to only users that match the specified status: - <code>ActivationRequired</code>: Membership Activation required - <code>ActivationSent</code>: Membership activation sent to user - <code>Active</code>: User Membership is active - <code>Closed</code>: User Membership is closed - <code>Disabled</code>: User Membership is disabled</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of folder IDs for which to return shared item information. If <code>item_type</code> is set to <code>folders</code>, at least one folder ID is required.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of user IDs for whom the shared item information is being requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Item type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the type of shared item being requested. The possible values are: - <code>envelopes</code>: Get information about envelope sharing between users. - <code>templates</code>: Get information about template sharing among users and groups. - <code>folders</code>: Get information about folder sharing among users and groups.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the number of responses is greater than <code>count</code>, this specifies the number of responses to skip. Typically this value is a multiple of <code>count</code>. The default is 0.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Signing Groups Get Signing Groups

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-signing-groups-by-account-id>`

Retrieves a list of all signing groups in the specified account. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/signing_groups endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Group type</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include users</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the response includes the signing group members.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/signing_groups/{signingGroupId} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Signing Group Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. ## Supported Languages Get Supported Languages

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-supported-languages-by-account-id>`

Retrieves a list of supported languages that you can set for an individual recipient when creating
an envelope, as well as their simple type enumeration values. These are the languages that you can
set for the standard email format and signing view for each recipient. For example, in the
recipientâs email notification, this setting affects elements such as the standard introductory text
describing the request to sign. It also determines the language used for buttons and tabs in both
the email notification and the signing experience.

**Note**: Setting a language for a recipient affects only the DocuSign standard text. Any custom
text that you enter for the `emailBody` and `emailSubject` of the notification is not translated,
and appears exactly as you enter it.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/supported_languages
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Tabs Get Tab Definitions

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-tab-definitions-by-account-id>`

Retrieves a list of all tabs associated with the account. This operation makes an HTTP GET request
to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/tab_definitions endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom tab only</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, only custom tabs are returned in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Tab Get Custom Tab

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-tab-definitions-by-account-id-custom-tab-id>`

Retrieves information about the requested custom tab on the specified account. This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/tab_definitions/{customTabId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Tab Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The DocuSign-generated custom tab ID for the custom tab to be applied. This can only be used when adding new tabs for a recipient. When used, the new tab inherits all the custom tab properties.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Templates Get Templates

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-templates-by-account-id>`

Retrieves the list of templates for the specified account. The request can be limited to a specific
folder. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/templates
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Shared by me</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, the response only includes templates shared by the user. If false, the response only returns template not shared by the user. If not specified, the response is not affected.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of records to return in the cache.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Is download</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, downloads the templates listed in <code>template_ids</code> as a collection of JSON definitions in a single zip file. The <code>Content-Disposition</code> header is set in the response. The value of the header provides the filename of the file. The default is <strong>false</strong>. <strong>Note</strong>: This parameter only works when you specify a list of templates in the <code>template_ids</code> parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Search text</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The search text to use to search the names of templates.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Used to date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End of the search date range. Only returns templates used or edited up to this date/time. If no value is provided, this defaults to the current date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Used from date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start of the search date range. Only returns templates used or edited on or after this date/time. If no value is specified, there is no limit on the earliest date used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of template ids to download. This value is valid only when <code>is_download</code> is <strong>true</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Search fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of additional template properties to search. - <code>sender</code>: Include sender name and email in the search. - <code>recipients</code>: Include recipient names and emails in the search. - <code>envelope</code>: Not used in template searches.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder types</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of folder to return templates for. Possible values are: - <code>templates</code>: Templates in the <strong>My Templates</strong> folder. Templates in the <strong>Shared Templates</strong> and <strong>All Template</strong> folders (if the request ID from and Admin) are excluded. - <code>templates_root</code>: Templates in the root level of the <strong>My Templates</strong> folder, but not in an actual folder. Note that the <strong>My Templates</strong> folder is not a real folder. - <code>recylebin</code>: Templates that have been deleted.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created to date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Lists templates modified before this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created from date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Lists templates created on or after this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters the templates in the response. Valid values are: - <code>owned_by_me</code>: Results include only templates owned by the user. - <code>shared_with_me</code>: Results include only templates owned by the user. - <code>all</code>: Results include all templates owned or shared with the user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>order</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the sort order of the search results. Valid values are: - <code>asc</code>: Ascending (A to Z) - <code>desc</code>: Descending (Z to A)</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Order by</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies how the search results are listed. Valid values are: - <code>name</code>: template name - <code>modified</code>: date/time template was last modified - <code>used</code>: date/time the template was last used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>include</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of additional template attributes to include in the response. Valid values are: - <code>powerforms</code>: Includes details about the PowerForms associated with the templates. - <code>documents</code>: Includes information about template documents. - <code>folders</code>: Includes information about the folder that holds the template. - <code>favorite_template_status</code>: Includes the template <code>favoritedByMe</code> property. <strong>Note</strong>: You can mark a template as a favorite only in eSignature v2.1. - <code>advanced_templates</code>: Includes information about advanced templates. - <code>recipients</code>: Includes information about template recipients. - <code>custom_fields</code>: Includes information about template custom fields. - <code>notifications</code>: Includes information about the notification settings for templates.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>From date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start of the search date range. Only returns templates created on or after this date/time. If no value is specified, there is no limit on the earliest date created.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of folder ID GUIDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Modified to date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Lists templates modified before this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The starting zero-based index position for the first template to show in the response. This value must be greater than or equal to <code>0</code> (zero).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Modified from date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Lists templates modified on or after this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>To date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The end of a search date range in UTC DateTime format. When you use this parameter, only templates created up to this date and time are returned. <strong>Note</strong>: If this property is null, the value defaults to the current date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Custom Fields Get Template Custom Fields

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-templates-custom-fields-by-account-id-template-id>`

Retrieves the custom document field information from an existing template. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/templates/{templateId}/custom_fields endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the template.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Documents Get Template Documents

## Documents Get Template Document

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-templates-documents-by-account-id-template-id-document-id>`

This method retrieves one or more PDF documents from the template that you specify. You can specify
the ID of the document to retrieve, or pass in the value `combined` to retrieve all documents in the
template as a single PDF file. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/templates/{templateId}/documents/{documentId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The <code>documentId</code> is set by the API client. It is an integer that falls between <code>1</code> and 2,147,483,647. The value is encoded as a string without commas. The values <code>1</code>, <code>2</code>, <code>3</code>, and so on are typically used to identify the first few documents in an envelope. Tab definitions include a <code>documentId</code> property that specifies the document on which to place the tab.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the template.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>encrypt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the PDF bytes returned in the response are encrypted for all the key managers configured on your DocuSign account. You can decrypt the documents by using the Key Manager DecryptDocument API method. For more information about Key Manager, see the DocuSign Security Appliance Installation Guide that your organization received from DocuSign.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show changes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, any document fields that a recipient changed are highlighted in yellow in the returned PDF document, and optional signatures or initials are outlined in red.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Pages Get Template Page Images

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-templates-documents-pages-by-account-id-template-id-document-id>`

Returns images of the pages in a template document for display based on the parameters that you
specify. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/templates/{templateId}/documents/{documentId}/pages endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>(Required) The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>(Required) The ID of the document.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>(Required) The ID of the template.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max width</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the maximum width of the returned images in pixels.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of results to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max height</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sets the maximum height of the returned images in pixels.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>dpi</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of dots per inch (DPI) for the resulting images. Valid values are 1-310 DPI. The default value is 94.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>nocache</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <strong>true</strong>, using cache is disabled and image information is retrieved from a database. <strong>True</strong> is the default value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show changes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <strong>true</strong>, changes display in the user interface.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The position within the total result set from which to start returning values. The value <strong>thumbnail</strong> may be used to return the page image.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Pages Get Template Page Image

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-templates-documents-pages-page-image-by-account-id-template-id-document-id-page-number>`

Retrieves a page image for display from the specified template. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/templates/{templateId}/documents/{documentId}/pages/{pageNumber}/page_image
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The <code>documentId</code> is set by the API client. The values <code>1</code>, <code>2</code>, <code>3</code>, and so on are typically used to identify the first few documents in an envelope. Tab definitions include a <code>documentId</code> property that specifies the document on which to place the tab.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The page number being accessed.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the template.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>dpi</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of dots per inch (DPI) for the resulting images. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Lock Get Template Lock

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-templates-lock-by-account-id-template-id>`

Retrieves general information about the template lock. If the call is made by the user who has the
lock and the request has the same integration key as original, then the `X-DocuSign-Edit` header
field and additional lock information is included in the response. This enables users to recover a
lost editing session token and the `X-DocuSign-Edit` header.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/templates/{templateId}/lock endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the template.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Recipients Get Template Bulk Recipients

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-templates-recipients-bulk-recipients-by-account-id-template-id-recipient-id>`

Retrieves the bulk recipient file information from a template that has a bulk recipient. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/templates/{templateId}/recipients/{recipientId}/bulk_recipients endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recipient Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A local reference that senders use to map recipients to other objects, such as specific document tabs. Within an envelope, each <code>recipientId</code> must be unique, but there is no uniqueness requirement across envelopes. For example, many envelopes assign the first recipient a <code>recipientId</code> of <code>1</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the template.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include tabs</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the tab information associated with the recipient is included in the response. ## Recipients Get Template Recipients

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-templates-recipients-by-account-id-template-id>`

Retrieves the information for all recipients in the specified template. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/templates/{templateId}/recipients endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the template.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include anchor tab locations</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong> and <code>include_tabs</code> is set to <strong>true</strong>, all tabs with anchor tab properties are included in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include extended</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the extended properties are included in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include tabs</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the tab information associated with the recipient is included in the response. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Recipients Get Template Recipient Document Visibility

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-templates-recipients-document-visibility-by-account-id-template-id-recipient-id>`

makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/templates/{templateId}/recipients/{recipientId}/document_visibility
endpoint.

## Unsupported File Types Get Unsupported File Types

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-unsupported-file-types-by-account-id>`

through the DocuSign system. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/unsupported_file_types endpoint.

## Users Get Users

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-users-by-account-id>`

Retrieves the list of users for the specified account. The response returns the list of users for
the account, with information about the result set. If the `additional_info` query is added to the
endpoint and set to **true**, full user information is returned for each user. This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/users endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>(Required) The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results based on one or more group IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of records to return. This number must be greater than <code>0</code> and less than or equal to <code>100</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>email</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results based on the email address associated with the user that you want to return. <strong>Note</strong>: You can use either this parameter or the <code>email_substring</code> parameter, but not both. For older accounts, this parameter might return multiple users who are associated with a single email address.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include usersettings for csv</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the response includes the <code>userSettings</code> object data in CSV format.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Email substring</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results based on a fragment of an email address. For example, you could enter <code>gmail</code> to return all users who have Gmail addresses. <strong>Note</strong>: You do not use a wildcard character with this parameter. You can use either this parameter or the <code>email</code> parameter, but not both.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional info</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the custom settings information is returned for each user in the account. If this parameter is omitted, the default behavior is <strong>false</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When set to <strong>true</strong>, the response includes the login status of each user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User name substring</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results based on a full or partial user name. <strong>Note</strong>: When you enter a partial user name, you do not use a wildcard character.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Not group ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Return user records excluding the specified group IDs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>status</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results by user account status. Possible values are: * <code>ActivationRequired</code> * <code>ActivationSent</code> * <code>Active</code> * <code>Closed</code> * <code>Disabled</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The position within the total result set from which to start returning values.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## User Get User

`<docusign:get-v21-accounts-users-by-account-id-user-id>`

Retrieves the user information for the specified user. To return additional user information that
details the last login date, login status, and the userâs password expiration date, set the optional
`additional_info` query string parameter to `true`. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/users/{userId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The external account number (int) or account ID GUID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the user to access. Generally this is the ID of the current authenticated user, but if the authenticated user is an Administrator on the account, <code>userId</code> can represent another user whom the Administrator is accessing.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Additional info</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Setting this parameter has no effect in this operation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>email</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Setting this parameter has no effect in this operation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Response Timeout field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming.
